title: DoorDash's Blueprint for Profitability in 2025 and Beyond
text: DoorDash faces ongoing regulatory challenges that impact profitability. In Seattle, the company operated at a loss in 2024 due to costly regulations requiring platforms to pay delivery workers nearly $30 an hour before mileage and tips, well above the city's minimum wage. In New York City, Dashers must earn at least $29.93 per hour of active time, nearly twice NYC's $15 minimum wage for other workers. These regulations significantly increase the costs of facilitating delivery and have forced DoorDash to raise fees for consumers in affected cities. Average fees for Seattle consumers are the highest in the country, over two times higher than average fees in similar cities like Denver, San Francisco, and Portland. On a typical order, approximately 70-80% goes back to restaurants and 15-20% or more goes to drivers, leaving DoorDash with minimal margins. These fundamental cost structures and regulatory pressures make achieving profitability on a per-order basis incredibly difficult, requiring DoorDash to focus on higher-margin business lines like advertising, subscriptions, and retail.
